channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: A Reliability Evaluation of Hybrid Deterministic-LLM Based Approaches for Academic Course Registration PDF Information Extraction
item.description: arXiv:2604.00003v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This study evaluates the reliability of information extraction approaches from KRS documents using three strategies: LLM only, Hybrid Deterministic - LLM (regex + LLM), and a Camelot based pipeline with LLM fallback. Experiments were conducted on 140 ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, study, arxiv, LLM, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: How Trustworthy Are LLM-as-Judge Ratings for Interpretive Responses? Implications for Qualitative Research Workflows
item.description: arXiv:2604.00008v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As qualitative researchers show growing interest in using automated tools to support interpretive analysis, a large language model (LLM) is often introduced into an analytic workflow as is, without systematic evaluation of interpretive quality or comp...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> GPT, model, tool, framework, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Eyla: Toward an Identity-Anchored LLM Architecture with Integrated Biological Priors -- Vision, Implementation Attempt, and Lessons from AI-Assisted Development
item.description: arXiv:2604.00009v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present the design rationale, implementation attempt, and failure analysis of Eyla, a proposed identity-anchored LLM architecture that integrates biologically-inspired subsystems -- including HiPPO-initialized state-space models, zero-initialized a...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, model, arxiv, vision, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Can LLMs Perceive Time? An Empirical Investigation
item.description: arXiv:2604.00010v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models cannot estimate how long their own tasks take. We investigate this limitation through four experiments across 68 tasks and four model families. Pre-task estimates overshoot actual duration by 4--7$\times$ ($p < 0.001$), with mode...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> GPT, model, arxiv, LLM, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Finding and Reactivating Post-Trained LLMs' Hidden Safety Mechanisms
item.description: arXiv:2604.00012v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite the impressive performance of general-purpose large language models (LLMs), they often require fine-tuning or post-training to excel at specific tasks. For instance, large reasoning models (LRMs), such as the DeepSeek-R1 series, demonstrate st...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, reasoning, fine-tuning, arxiv, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: How Emotion Shapes the Behavior of LLMs and Agents: A Mechanistic Study
item.description: arXiv:2604.00005v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Emotion plays an important role in human cognition and performance. Motivated by this, we investigate whether analogous emotional signals can shape the behavior of large language models (LLMs) and agents. Existing emotion-aware studies mainly treat em...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, reasoning, framework, study, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Safety-Aware Role-Orchestrated Multi-Agent LLM Framework for Behavioral Health Communication Simulation
item.description: arXiv:2604.00249v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Single-agent large language model (LLM) systems struggle to simultaneously support diverse conversational functions and maintain safety in behavioral health communication. We propose a safety-aware, role-orchestrated multi-agent LLM framework designed...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, tool, framework, arxiv, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Efficient and Principled Scientific Discovery through Bayesian Optimization: A Tutorial
item.description: arXiv:2604.01328v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Traditional scientific discovery relies on an iterative hypothesise-experiment-refine cycle that has driven progress for centuries, but its intuitive, ad-hoc implementation often wastes resources, yields inefficient designs, and misses critical insigh...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, arxiv, context, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: In harmony with gpt-oss
item.description: arXiv:2604.00362v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: No one has independently reproduced OpenAI's published scores for gpt-oss-20b with tools, because the original paper discloses neither the tools nor the agent harness. We reverse-engineered the model's in-distribution tools: when prompted without tool...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> GPT, model, tool, arxiv, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DySCo: Dynamic Semantic Compression for Effective Long-term Time Series Forecasting
item.description: arXiv:2604.01261v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Time series forecasting (TSF) is critical across domains such as finance, meteorology, and energy. While extending the lookback window theoretically provides richer historical context, in practice, it often introduces irrelevant noise and computationa...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, compression, framework, arxiv, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Detecting Complex Money Laundering Patterns with Incremental and Distributed Graph Modeling
item.description: arXiv:2604.01315v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Money launderers take advantage of limitations in existing detection approaches by hiding their financial footprints in a deceitful manner. They manage this by replicating transaction patterns that the monitoring systems cannot easily distinguish. As ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> attention, model, framework, release, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SECURE: Stable Early Collision Understanding via Robust Embeddings in Autonomous Driving
item.description: arXiv:2604.01337v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While deep learning has significantly advanced accident anticipation, the robustness of these safety-critical systems against real-world perturbations remains a major challenge. We reveal that state-of-the-art models like CRASH, despite their high per...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, arxiv, embedding, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: An Online Machine Learning Multi-resolution Optimization Framework for Energy System Design Limit of Performance Analysis
item.description: arXiv:2604.01308v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Designing reliable integrated energy systems for industrial processes requires optimization and verification models across multiple fidelities, from architecture-level sizing to high-fidelity dynamic operation. However, model mismatch across fidelitie...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, study, framework, arxiv, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: JetPrism: diagnosing convergence for generative simulation and inverse problems in nuclear physics
item.description: arXiv:2604.01313v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: High-fidelity Monte Carlo simulations and complex inverse problems, such as mapping smeared experimental observations to ground-truth states, are computationally intensive yet essential for robust data analysis. Conditional Flow Matching (CFM) offers ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, framework, arxiv, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: UQ-SHRED: uncertainty quantification of shallow recurrent decoder networks for sparse sensing via engression
item.description: arXiv:2604.01305v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reconstructing high-dimensional spatiotemporal fields from sparse sensor measurements is critical in a wide range of scientific applications. The SHallow REcurrent Decoder (SHRED) architecture is a recent state-of-the-art architecture that reconstruct...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, arxiv, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
